title: Outer rise seismicity related to the Maule, Chile 2010 mega-thrust earthquake and hydration of the incoming oceanic lithosphere
abstract: During the first weeks following the 2010 Maule main shock, an outer rise seismic cluster of >450 events (mainly extensional, Mw=4–6) formed seaward of the main slip maximum. Outer rise seismicity is strongly correlated with the heterogeneous coseismic slip distribution. Seismic constraints reveal reduced P-wave velocities in the uppermost mantle at the trench-outer rise region (7.5–7.8 km/s), suggesting serpentinization. High-resolution bathymetry reveals bending-related faulting more intense seaward of maximum slip.

location.description: Outer rise of the incoming Nazca plate offshore Maule earthquake zone (~34°–36°S)

seismic_lines.name: Wide-angle OBS refraction profile — Maule outer rise ~34°–36°S

data.name: OBS Wide-Angle Data — Maule Outer Rise ~34°–36°S
data.description: Wide-angle OBS refraction data from post-Maule cruise at the outer rise (~34°–36°S); P-wave velocities 7.5–7.8 km/s in uppermost mantle indicating serpentinization; combined with aftershock catalog

data.name: High-Resolution Bathymetry — Maule Outer Rise
data.description: High-resolution multibeam bathymetry revealing bending-related fault intensity correlated with coseismic slip distribution of Maule earthquake

_data_note: Andean Geology open access journal. Post-Maule survey data. Specific cruise not confirmed from available metadata.

processing.summary: Wide-angle OBS seismic refraction data from the outer rise offshore Maule (~34°–36°S) were processed to derive 2D P-wave velocity models revealing serpentinization (Vp 7.5–7.8 km/s). Combined with post-Maule aftershock catalog (>450 events) and high-resolution bathymetry showing bending-related faults more intense seaward of maximum slip. Published in Andean Geology (open access).
workflow: 1. OBS data processing and instrument relocation
5. Correlation with bathymetry and slip model
processing.notes: Andean Geology open access paper; post-Maule earthquake survey

analysis_notes: Open access via Andean Geology (andeangeology.cl). This paper uses published earthquake catalog data (USGS/CMT) and published seismic velocity constraints from prior studies — no new seismic acquisition by these authors. Vessel and year_acquired set to null. Outer rise seismicity catalog + bathymetry compiled from prior surveys.
